Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. In Memoriam: John W. Addison, my PhD advisor 109 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. LLM Architecture Gallery 313 upvotes, 23 comments
  3. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 338 upvotes, 29 comments
  4. Bandit: A 32bit baremetal computer that runs Color Forth [video] 47 upvotes, 2 comments
  5. The Linux Programming Interface as a university course text 61 upvotes, 5 comments
  6. How I write software with LLMs 52 upvotes, 6 comments
  7. A Plain Anabaptist Story: The Hutterites 35 upvotes, 3 comments
  8. Learning athletic humanoid tennis skills from imperfect human motion data 135 upvotes, 27 comments
  9. Electric motor scaling laws and inertia in robot actuators 24 upvotes, 2 comments
  10. Canada's bill C-22 mandates mass metadata surveillance of Canadians 536 upvotes, 145 comments
  11. What every computer scientist should know about floating-point arithmetic (1991) [pdf] 22 upvotes, 2 comments
  12. Nasdaq's Shame 251 upvotes, 83 comments
  13. SpiceCrypt: A Python library for decrypting LTspice encrypted model files 18 upvotes, 2 comments
  14. Chrome DevTools MCP (2025) 415 upvotes, 181 comments
  15. Bus travel from Lima to Rio de Janeiro 142 upvotes, 57 comments
  16. //go:fix inline and the source-level inliner 126 upvotes, 51 comments
  17. Separating the Wayland compositor and window manager 254 upvotes, 121 comments
  18. The 49MB web page 394 upvotes, 200 comments
  19. Stop Sloppypasta 201 upvotes, 98 comments
  20. Lies I was told about collaborative editing, Part 2: Why we don't use Yjs 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  21. Glassworm is back: A new wave of invisible Unicode attacks hits repositories 241 upvotes, 150 comments
  22. What is agentic engineering? 114 upvotes, 65 comments
  23. Type systems are leaky abstractions: the case of Map.take!/2 40 upvotes, 19 comments
  24. Cannabinoids remove plaque-forming Alzheimer's proteins from brain cells (2016) 88 upvotes, 51 comments
  25. What makes Intel Optane stand out (2023) 193 upvotes, 133 comments
  26. Federal Right to Privacy Act – Draft legislation 52 upvotes, 29 comments
  27. The emergence of print-on-demand Amazon paperback books 120 upvotes, 81 comments
  28. LLMs can be exhausting 142 upvotes, 109 comments
  29. An experiment to use GitHub Actions as a control plane for a PaaS 13 upvotes, 5 comments
  30. Quillx is an open standard for disclosing AI involvement in software projects 13 upvotes, 13 comments

Last generated 1:30pm, 16 March 2026. Source here.
